ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag from vision pass. Card depicts a generic vehicle wreck with no figures; no external IP risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift (set code DFT, released 2025-02-14) is the multi-plane death-race set staging the Ghirapur Grand Prix across Avishkar (renamed Kaladesh), Amonkhet, and Muraganda, with ten competing teams [Scryfall API: dft set]. Development codename was 'Tennis,' and Mark Rosewater publicly framed the set as a Death Race premise — Tumblr replies cite Death Race 2000, Wacky Races, and Fast and the Furious as touchstones [MTG Wiki: Aetherdrift, citing Mark Rosewater Blogatog posts 2023-08-06 and 2023-09-29].

Flavor text reads: "Under moonfall conditions, there's nothing you can do but drive and hope for the best." [Scryfall flavor_text]. 'Moonfall' is not generic weather flavor — it is a literal Muraganda condition: per MTG Wiki, 'the moons of Muraganda are unstable, and chunks of them float in the air and regularly crash on the plane,' and 'polished moonfall is used as a currency on Muraganda' [MTG Wiki: Muraganda]. The line situates the depicted wreck on Stage 3 of the Grand Prix, the Muraganda leg, where falling lunar debris is part of the racing hazard.

Aetherdrift story author K. Arsenault Rivera cited Fast and the Furious, Redline, Mad Max, and Speed Racer as her main touchstones for the set's racing voice [Magic Untapped: 'An Interview With Aetherdrift Story Author K. Arsenault Rivera', via WebSearch snippet]. Art on this card is by Nicholas Gregory, an illustrator credited with 121 Magic cards on Gatherer [Gatherer artist search: Nicholas Gregory; mtg.wtf artist page]. The card is uncommon, single printing, no reprints [Scryfall prints_search].

A {1}{R} instant that destroys target artifact, with Cycling {R} [Scryfall oracle_text]. Draftsim's Aetherdrift Limited review describes it as 'dirt cheap cycling on a Shatter' that is 'amazing in a set like this,' noting 'you'll often have good targets for your Skycrash, but pay virtually zero cost when you don't' [Draftsim: 'The Ultimate Aetherdrift Limited Set Review', via WebSearch snippet]. Commander coverage frames it as a 'flexible utility card' worth running where the format presents fast artifact threats [Commander's Herald: 'Aetherdrift Set Review - Red', via WebSearch snippet]. EDHREC rank 13,550 places it well outside Commander staple territory [Scryfall: edhrec_rank]. Standard / Pioneer / Modern / Legacy / Vintage / Commander legal; Pauper not legal despite the uncommon-only printing [Scryfall legalities].

Cycling is one of Aetherdrift's six returning/new mechanics in the set alongside Crew, Exhaust, Max Speed, Saddle, and Start your engines! [MTG Wiki: Aetherdrift, Themes and mechanics box]. Aetherdrift is the third set in the Dragonstorm Arc [MTG Wiki: Aetherdrift, Storyline section].

related_cards.name: Muraganda Raceway
related_cards.note: The land card for Stage 3 of the Grand Prix; Skycrash's 'moonfall' flavor text places the depicted wreck on Muraganda's track.

related_cards.name: Avishkar Raceway
related_cards.note: Stage 1 & 5 raceway land — companion entry in the Raceway land cycle.

related_cards.name: Amonkhet Raceway
related_cards.note: Stage 2 & 4 raceway land — companion entry in the Raceway land cycle.

related_cards.name: Shatter
related_cards.note: Mechanical antecedent — Skycrash is functionally Shatter with cycling stapled on; cited as the comparison point in Draftsim's Limited review.

related_cards.name: Fuel the Flames
related_cards.note: Set-mate red instant by the same artist (Nicholas Gregory) — another cycling-bearing red removal piece in the same packfile.
